message.content:
**Considering file deletion method**

I'm figuring out how to compute planned deletions and show them in a diff format before applying changes. The instructions say to use editing tools instead of terminal commands. However, I realized that I could delete files via PowerShell with `Remove-Item`. The user wants a comparison before applying, so I'll present a simulated diff, possibly using `git diff`, but I may also explore `git rm --dry-run` for computing the diff without making changes. It's all about accuracy!**Considering file deletion method**

I'm figuring out how to compute planned deletions and show them in a diff format before applying changes. The instructions say to use editing tools instead of terminal commands. However, I realized that I could delete files via PowerShell with `Remove-Item`. The user wants a comparison before applying, so I'll present a simulated diff, possibly using `git diff`, but I may also explore `git rm --dry-run` for computing the diff without making changes. It's all about accuracy!**Analyzing git deletion process**

I'm looking into how to show file deletions with git. It seems `git rm -n` indicates what would be removed, but it doesn't provide a diff. To generate a diff for file deletions, I consider using `git diff --no-index /dev/null file` to create a deletion patch without actually deleting the file. This means I would run `git diff --no-index -- path /dev/null` for each file, but I'll need to ensure I get this process right.

I think I also need to create a list of safe files to delete and maybe generate a cleanup report in JSON or Markdown format! I'll look into searching for "cleanup report" while using glob and grep in parallel.**Analyzing git deletion process**

I'm looking into how to show file deletions with git. It seems `git rm -n` indicates what would be removed, but it doesn't provide a diff. To generate a diff for file deletions, I consider using `git diff --no-index /dev/null file` to create a deletion patch without actually deleting the file. This means I would run `git diff --no-index -- path /dev/null` for each file, but I'll need to ensure I get this process right.

I think I also need to create a list of safe files to delete and maybe generate a cleanup report in JSON or Markdown format! I'll look into searching for "cleanup report" while using glob and grep in parallel.

function.name: run_terminal_command
function.description:
Run a terminal command in the current directory.
The shell is not stateful and will not remember any previous commands.      When a command is run in the background ALWAYS suggest using shell commands to stop it; NEVER suggest using Ctrl+C.      When suggesting subsequent shell commands ALWAYS format them in shell command blocks.      Do NOT perform actions requiring special/admin privileges.      IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.

command.description: The command to run. This will be passed directly into the IDE shell.

waitForCompletion.description: Whether to wait for the command to complete before returning. Default is true. Set to false to run the command in the background. Set to true to run the command in the foreground and wait to collect the output.

IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.
You can also optionally include the waitForCompletion argument set to false to run the command in the background.

function.name: run_terminal_command
function.description:
Run a terminal command in the current directory.
The shell is not stateful and will not remember any previous commands.      When a command is run in the background ALWAYS suggest using shell commands to stop it; NEVER suggest using Ctrl+C.      When suggesting subsequent shell commands ALWAYS format them in shell command blocks.      Do NOT perform actions requiring special/admin privileges.      IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.

command.description: The command to run. This will be passed directly into the IDE shell.

waitForCompletion.description: Whether to wait for the command to complete before returning. Default is true. Set to false to run the command in the background. Set to true to run the command in the foreground and wait to collect the output.

To run a terminal command, use the run_terminal_command tool

IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.
You can also optionally include the waitForCompletion argument set to false to run the command in the background.

function.name: run_terminal_command
function.description:
Run a terminal command in the current directory.
The shell is not stateful and will not remember any previous commands.      When a command is run in the background ALWAYS suggest using shell commands to stop it; NEVER suggest using Ctrl+C.      When suggesting subsequent shell commands ALWAYS format them in shell command blocks.      Do NOT perform actions requiring special/admin privileges.      IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.

command.description: The command to run. This will be passed directly into the IDE shell.

waitForCompletion.description: Whether to wait for the command to complete before returning. Default is true. Set to false to run the command in the background. Set to true to run the command in the foreground and wait to collect the output.

systemMessageDescription.prefix:
To run a terminal command, use the run_terminal_command tool
The shell is not stateful and will not remember any previous commands.      When a command is run in the background ALWAYS suggest using shell commands to stop it; NEVER suggest using Ctrl+C.      When suggesting subsequent shell commands ALWAYS format them in shell command blocks.      Do NOT perform actions requiring special/admin privileges.      IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.
You can also optionally include the waitForCompletion argument set to false to run the command in the background.

function.name: run_terminal_command
function.arguments: {"command":"powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command \"$root=(Get-Location).Path; $safe=@('stderr.log','truncate()'); $safe += (Get-ChildItem -File -Path 'storage/logs' -Filter '*.log' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName.Substring($root.Length+1) }); if(Test-Path 'public/C_'){ $safe += (Get-ChildItem -File -Recurse -Path 'public/C_' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName.Substring($root.Length+1) }) }; $safe = $safe | Sort-Object -Unique; $safe\"","waitForCompletion":true}

function.name: run_terminal_command
function.arguments: {"command":"powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command \"$root=(Get-Location).Path; $safe=@('stderr.log','truncate()'); $safe += (Get-ChildItem -File -Path 'storage/logs' -Filter '*.log' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName.Substring($root.Length+1) }); if(Test-Path 'public/C_'){ $safe += (Get-ChildItem -File -Recurse -Path 'public/C_' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName.Substring($root.Length+1) }) }; $safe = $safe | Sort-Object -Unique; $safe\"","waitForCompletion":true}

parsedArgs.command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "$root=(Get-Location).Path; $safe=@('stderr.log','truncate()'); $safe += (Get-ChildItem -File -Path 'storage/logs' -Filter '*.log' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName.Substring($root.Length+1) }); if(Test-Path 'public/C_'){ $safe += (Get-ChildItem -File -Recurse -Path 'public/C_' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.FullName.Substring($root.Length+1) }) }; $safe = $safe | Sort-Object -Unique; $safe"

function.name: run_terminal_command
function.description:
Run a terminal command in the current directory.
The shell is not stateful and will not remember any previous commands.      When a command is run in the background ALWAYS suggest using shell commands to stop it; NEVER suggest using Ctrl+C.      When suggesting subsequent shell commands ALWAYS format them in shell command blocks.      Do NOT perform actions requiring special/admin privileges.      IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.

command.description: The command to run. This will be passed directly into the IDE shell.

waitForCompletion.description: Whether to wait for the command to complete before returning. Default is true. Set to false to run the command in the background. Set to true to run the command in the foreground and wait to collect the output.

systemMessageDescription.prefix:
To run a terminal command, use the run_terminal_command tool
The shell is not stateful and will not remember any previous commands.      When a command is run in the background ALWAYS suggest using shell commands to stop it; NEVER suggest using Ctrl+C.      When suggesting subsequent shell commands ALWAYS format them in shell command blocks.      Do NOT perform actions requiring special/admin privileges.      IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.
You can also optionally include the waitForCompletion argument set to false to run the command in the background.      
For example, to see the git log, you could respond with:

function.name: run_terminal_command
function.description:
Run a terminal command in the current directory.
The shell is not stateful and will not remember any previous commands.      When a command is run in the background ALWAYS suggest using shell commands to stop it; NEVER suggest using Ctrl+C.      When suggesting subsequent shell commands ALWAYS format them in shell command blocks.      Do NOT perform actions requiring special/admin privileges.      IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for win32 and x64 and shell powershell.exe.

command.description: The command to run. This will be passed directly into the IDE shell.

waitForCompletion.description: Whether to wait for the command to complete before returning. Default is true. Set to false to run the command in the background. Set to true to run the command in the foreground and wait to collect the output.

message.content:
```shell
powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "Write-Host 'storage/logs/*.log'; Get-ChildItem -Path 'storage/logs' -Filter '*.log' -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Name,Length,LastWriteTime | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Host ''; if (Test-Path 'public/C_') { Write-Host 'public/C_ (recursive)'; Get-ChildItem -Path 'public/C_' -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Mode,Length,LastWriteTime,FullName | Format-Table -AutoSize } else { Write-Host 'public/C_ does not exist' }"
```
